title: Brain reliance map
description: Backing JSON Schema for the hand-authored reliance map at .gaia/knowledge/brain-reliance-map.yaml — the single source of truth that declares, per workflow stage, which brain-knowledge nodes that stage relies upon and whether each reliance is mandatory or optional. Unlike the brain index, this file is hand-authored policy: the reindex sweep neither reads nor writes it. The `stages` map is keyed by a `<skill>:<stage-id>` composite (the workflow stage a loader is entering); each value carries a `requires` list of reliance entries. Every reliance entry names one `brain_node` (the key of the required node, looked up in the brain index) and an `obligation` drawn from the CLOSED enum {MANDATORY, OPTIONAL}. additionalProperties is false on each reliance entry so an out-of-enum obligation or a stray field is rejected; the stage object and the document root stay permissive enough for the keyed-map shape and forward-compatible headers.

stages.description: Map of workflow stages to their brain-node reliances. Each key is a `<skill>:<stage-id>` composite identifying the stage a workflow-entry loader is entering; an absent key means the stage declares no reliances and is un-evaluable (the loader fails open on it).

additionalProperties.description: The reliance declaration for a single stage.

requires.description: The list of brain nodes this stage relies upon. May be empty: a stage with no requirements is trivially satisfied.

items.description: A single reliance entry: one required brain node plus its obligation.

brain_node.description: The key of the required brain node, resolved against the brain index at load time.

obligation.description: Closed enum. MANDATORY: a cleanly-evaluated absence of this node HALTs the entering workflow. OPTIONAL: an absence is a warning and the workflow continues. A value outside this enum is a schema violation.
